key_insight: High-velocity air fuel (HVAF) spraying can produce thick NiTi coatings that retain martensitic transformation and shape memory functionality, unlike many prior coating routes.
engineering_relevance: This enables wear- and corrosion-resistant NiTi surface layers for engineering components while preserving thermal actuation and shape-memory behavior.
method: HVAF-sprayed NiTi onto mild steel in 4 processing-parameter sets
Characterized composition, porosity, microstructure, phase transformation, and thermomechanical response
Assessed functionality using 3-point bending, nanoindentation, and scratch tests
evidence_signal: The coatings were evaluated as 100-300 um thick layers and showed martensitic transformation on cooling/mechanical loading, plus thermal actuation and shape memory in mechanical tests.
watch_item: As-sprayed coatings are microstructurally inhomogeneous and require annealing; tensile strength remains limited.
paper_title: HVAF Spraying of NiTi Coatings: Microstructure, Phase Transformation and Shape Memory Behavior
